page: Microsoft Azure NC16as_T4_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine belonging to the NCASv3_T4 family. It is powered by an AMD processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs. The virtualization layer is managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 110.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 6.88 GB of RAM per core.

This server features a bundled NVIDIA T4 graphics processing unit with 15 GB of dedicated VRAM, designed to accelerate parallel computing tasks. For local storage, the instance includes a 360 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The combination of dedicated AMD vCPUs, a substantial memory footprint, and an NVIDIA T4 GPU makes this instance suitable for workloads such as machine learning inference, small-scale deep learning training, video encoding, and graphics rendering. The lack of advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 or AMX in the provided hardware profile suggests that compute tasks relying heavily on these specific CPU vector extensions may experience performance limitations compared to newer architectures.
description: Microsoft Azure NC16as_T4_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine from the NCASv3_T4 family, powered by an AMD x86_64 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs. It features 110.0 GB of system memory, providing 6.88 GB of RAM per core, and is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The hardware configuration includes a single NVIDIA T4 GPU with 15 GB of VRAM, alongside 360 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. This server is designed for workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration, such as machine learning inference, analytical processing, and graphics rendering. It does not provide complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and its CPU profile lacks advanced vector extensions like AVX-512. This resource density offers a balanced profile for entry-level GPU workloads and parallel computing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure NC16as_T4_v3 is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 110 GB of RAM, a 15 GB NVIDIA T4 GPU, and 360 GB of local storage for parallel computing workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD processors, substantial memory capacity, and local storage for parallel computing workloads.

110.0 GB of system memory with 6.88 GB per core
One NVIDIA T4 GPU with 15 GB of VRAM
